key_insight: Plasma-sprayed Inconel 625 on T22 steel offers initial protection in nitrate salts at 500 °C, but the coating progressively degrades and eventually reaches the substrate interface.
engineering_relevance: This suggests In625 thermal-spray coatings can extend component life in concentrating solar power thermal energy storage systems, but long-term corrosion resistance remains a critical design concern.
method: Compact Plasma Spray deposited Inconel 625 onto T22 carbon steel.
Samples were exposed to eutectic nitrate salt at 500 °C.
Corrosion was assessed after 24, 72, 168, 336, and 504 hours.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that after 24 hours corrosion products were only on the surface, while longer exposure drove degradation through the coating thickness and the corrosion rate reached 0.4 mm/year.
watch_item: Low cohesion between sprayed splats may accelerate through-thickness corrosion and limit the coating’s durability in service.
paper_title: Corrosion Behavior of Plasma-Sprayed In625 Coatings Exposed to Nitrate Salts in Concentrating Solar Power Plants
